# Topic

A policy-memo style paper about pension de-risking and liquidity fragility.

# Core Question

Why can pension de-risking transform solvency risk into liquidity risk?

# Intended Thesis

De-risking through leveraged LDI strategies concentrates liquidity fragility.

# Paper Type

policy_design_memo

# Scope

- Period: 2020–2023
- Region: United Kingdom (gilt market)
- Actors: defined-benefit pension funds, LDI managers
- Mechanisms: leveraged LDI, collateral/margin calls

# Exclusions

- Do not generalize to all mature pension markets without explicit qualifiers.
- Do not give direct China policy prescriptions.

# Seed Claims

1. UK LDI strategies created margin-call liquidity pressure during rapid gilt yield movements.
2. Pension de-risking may reduce funding volatility while increasing liquidity fragility.
3. Solvency and liquidity risk are distinct risk categories that LDI links together.

# Known Sources

- Bank of England Financial Stability Report, November 2022

# Success Criteria

- A frozen question to thesis spine in which every edge is strong or explicitly conditional.
